patterns.text: list all disk partitions on the system
patterns.explanation: Displays partition tables for all disks

patterns.text: show partitions for a specific disk
patterns.explanation: Lists partition layout of the specified disk

patterns.text: open the partition editor for a disk
patterns.explanation: Enters interactive mode to create, delete, or modify partitions

patterns.text: dump partition table in sector format
patterns.explanation: Shows specific partition details with custom columns

patterns.text: partition a disk using GPT
patterns.explanation: Opens interactive GPT partitioning tool for the disk

patterns.text: list GPT partitions on a disk
patterns.explanation: Displays the GPT partition table

patterns.text: convert an MBR disk to GPT
patterns.explanation: Converts the partition table from MBR to GPT format

patterns.text: backup the GPT partition table
patterns.explanation: Saves the partition table to a file for recovery

patterns.text: clone partition layout from one disk to another
patterns.explanation: Replicates the partition scheme from sda to sdb

commands.name: parted
commands.description: A partition manipulation program supporting MBR and GPT

patterns.text: list all partitions on all disks
patterns.explanation: Displays partition tables for every disk in the system

patterns.text: create a new GPT partition table on a disk
patterns.explanation: Initializes the disk with a fresh GPT partition table

patterns.text: create a partition using the entire disk
patterns.explanation: Creates a single partition spanning the whole disk

patterns.text: resize a partition
patterns.explanation: Expands partition 2 to fill all available space

patterns.text: print partition information for a disk
patterns.explanation: Shows the partition table with sizes and filesystem types

commands.name: mkfs
commands.description: Build a Linux filesystem on a partition

patterns.text: format a partition as ext4
patterns.explanation: Creates an ext4 filesystem on the partition

patterns.text: create an XFS filesystem
patterns.explanation: Formats the partition with the XFS filesystem

patterns.text: format a partition as FAT32 for USB drives
patterns.explanation: Creates a FAT32 filesystem, compatible with most devices

patterns.text: create an ext4 filesystem with a label
patterns.explanation: Formats and sets a human-readable label on the filesystem

patterns.text: create a Btrfs filesystem
patterns.explanation: Formats the partition with the Btrfs copy-on-write filesystem

commands.name: fsck
commands.description: Check and repair a Linux filesystem

patterns.text: check a filesystem for errors
patterns.explanation: Scans the filesystem for corruption and reports issues

patterns.text: automatically repair filesystem errors
patterns.explanation: Fixes all errors automatically without prompting

patterns.text: check a filesystem without making changes
patterns.explanation: Performs a read-only check and reports errors without fixing

patterns.text: force a filesystem check even if it appears clean
patterns.explanation: Forces a check even if the filesystem is marked clean

patterns.text: check all filesystems listed in fstab
patterns.explanation: Runs checks on all filesystems in /etc/fstab

commands.name: mount
commands.description: Mount a filesystem to a directory

patterns.text: mount a partition to a directory
patterns.explanation: Attaches the filesystem on sdb1 to the /mnt/data mount point

patterns.text: mount an ISO image file
patterns.explanation: Mounts an ISO file as a loopback device

patterns.text: mount an NFS share
patterns.explanation: Mounts a remote NFS filesystem

patterns.text: mount a filesystem as read-only
patterns.explanation: Attaches the filesystem in read-only mode

patterns.text: show all currently mounted filesystems
patterns.explanation: Lists all mounted filesystems in a formatted table

patterns.text: unmount a filesystem
patterns.explanation: Detaches the filesystem from the mount point

patterns.text: force unmount a busy filesystem
patterns.explanation: Forces unmounting even if the filesystem is in use

patterns.text: lazy unmount a filesystem
patterns.explanation: Detaches immediately and cleans up when no longer busy

patterns.text: unmount all filesystems of a given type
patterns.explanation: Unmounts all mounted NFS filesystems

patterns.text: list all block devices in a tree format
patterns.explanation: Displays all disks and partitions in a hierarchical tree

patterns.text: show filesystems and mount points
patterns.explanation: Shows filesystem type, label, UUID, and mount point for each device

patterns.text: show disk sizes in bytes
patterns.explanation: Displays sizes in bytes instead of human-readable format

patterns.text: show specific columns only
patterns.explanation: Displays only the selected columns

patterns.text: list devices as a flat list without tree
patterns.explanation: Shows devices in a plain list format

commands.name: blkid
commands.description: Locate and print block device attributes

patterns.text: show UUIDs and filesystem types of all partitions
patterns.explanation: Lists all block devices with their UUIDs and filesystem info

patterns.text: get the UUID of a specific partition
patterns.explanation: Shows the UUID, type, and label for the partition

patterns.text: output only the UUID value
patterns.explanation: Prints just the UUID string, useful for scripting

patterns.text: find a device by its label
patterns.explanation: Returns the device path that has the given label

commands.name: tune2fs
commands.description: Adjust tunable parameters on ext2/ext3/ext4 filesystems

patterns.text: set a label on an ext4 partition
patterns.explanation: Assigns the label mydata to the filesystem

patterns.text: show filesystem parameters
patterns.explanation: Lists all tunable parameters and superblock information

patterns.text: set the maximum mount count before a fsck is forced
patterns.explanation: Forces a filesystem check after 50 mounts

patterns.text: set reserved space percentage for root
patterns.explanation: Reserves 1% of space for root instead of the default 5%

patterns.text: enable a filesystem feature
patterns.explanation: Adds the journal feature to convert ext2 to ext3

patterns.text: check and repair an ext4 filesystem
patterns.explanation: Forces a full check of the ext filesystem

patterns.text: automatically fix errors on an ext partition
patterns.explanation: Forces a check and answers yes to all repair questions

patterns.text: check an ext filesystem in read-only mode
patterns.explanation: Reports errors without making any changes

patterns.text: show progress during filesystem check
patterns.explanation: Displays a progress bar during the check

commands.name: badblocks
commands.description: Search a device for bad blocks

patterns.text: scan a disk for bad sectors
patterns.explanation: Performs a read-only scan and reports any bad blocks found

patterns.text: do a non-destructive read-write test for bad blocks
patterns.explanation: Tests blocks by writing and reading without destroying data

patterns.text: write bad blocks list to a file
patterns.explanation: Saves discovered bad block numbers to a file

patterns.text: show progress during a bad block scan
patterns.explanation: Displays scan progress with verbose output

patterns.text: test the read speed of a drive
patterns.explanation: Benchmarks cached reads and buffered disk reads

patterns.text: show drive information and settings
patterns.explanation: Displays detailed identification info from the drive

patterns.text: enable write caching on a drive
patterns.explanation: Turns on the write cache for improved performance

patterns.text: put a drive into standby mode
patterns.explanation: Sends the drive into low-power standby mode

patterns.text: check if a drive supports TRIM
patterns.explanation: Checks TRIM support for SSD maintenance

commands.name: smartctl
commands.description: Control and monitor SMART-enabled storage devices

patterns.text: check the health status of a drive
patterns.explanation: Reports the overall SMART health assessment

patterns.text: show all SMART information for a drive
patterns.explanation: Displays all SMART data including attributes and error logs

patterns.text: run a short self-test on a drive
patterns.explanation: Initiates a quick self-test that takes a few minutes

patterns.text: run a long self-test on a drive
patterns.explanation: Starts a thorough self-test that may take hours

patterns.text: view the results of the last self-test
patterns.explanation: Shows the self-test log with results

patterns.text: create a RAID 1 mirror array
patterns.explanation: Creates a mirrored RAID array from two partitions

patterns.text: check the status of all RAID arrays
patterns.explanation: Shows the state and progress of all MD arrays

patterns.text: show details of a RAID array
patterns.explanation: Displays configuration, state, and member disks

patterns.text: add a spare disk to a RAID array
patterns.explanation: Adds a hot spare or replacement disk to the array

patterns.text: remove a failed disk from a RAID array
patterns.explanation: Removes a failed member from the array

patterns.text: list all logical volumes
patterns.explanation: Shows all logical volumes with size and volume group

patterns.text: show detailed logical volume information
patterns.explanation: Displays all LVs including hidden ones with their physical devices

patterns.text: show logical volumes in a specific volume group
patterns.explanation: Lists only the logical volumes in the vg_data group

patterns.text: display LV attributes and segments
patterns.explanation: Shows comprehensive details about a specific logical volume

patterns.text: list all volume groups
patterns.explanation: Shows all volume groups with free space and LV count

patterns.text: show detailed volume group information
patterns.explanation: Displays comprehensive details for all volume groups

patterns.text: show free space in volume groups
patterns.explanation: Lists volume groups with their total and free space

patterns.text: create a new volume group
patterns.explanation: Creates a volume group spanning two physical volumes

patterns.text: list all physical volumes in LVM
patterns.explanation: Shows all physical volumes with their volume group and free space

patterns.text: show detailed physical volume information
patterns.explanation: Displays comprehensive details for all physical volumes

patterns.text: initialize a disk partition for LVM
patterns.explanation: Creates a physical volume on the partition for use with LVM

patterns.text: check the allocation map of a physical volume
patterns.explanation: Shows which extents are allocated to which logical volumes

commands.name: lvcreate
commands.description: Create a logical volume in a volume group

patterns.text: create a logical volume with a specific size
patterns.explanation: Creates a 50GB logical volume named lv_data

patterns.text: create a logical volume using all free space
patterns.explanation: Uses all remaining space in the volume group

patterns.text: create a thin-provisioned logical volume
patterns.explanation: Creates a thin LV that can grow up to 100GB on demand

patterns.text: create a snapshot of a logical volume
patterns.explanation: Creates a 10GB snapshot of the specified logical volume

patterns.text: resize a logical volume and its filesystem
patterns.explanation: Extends the logical volume by 20GB and resizes the filesystem

patterns.text: find what is taking up disk space interactively
patterns.explanation: Scans the root filesystem and shows an interactive size browser

patterns.text: analyze disk usage of the home directory
patterns.explanation: Scans your home directory for the largest files and folders

patterns.text: scan excluding certain directories
patterns.explanation: Skips the specified directories during the scan

patterns.text: export disk usage data to a file for later viewing
patterns.explanation: Saves the scan results to a JSON file

patterns.text: view a previously saved ncdu report
patterns.explanation: Opens a saved report without rescanning
